# Transcrição: 5. Not just the product

- **Fonte:** 5. Not just the product
- **Duração:** 02:47
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:47]

Okay, one of the biggest mistakes sales people make is they believe the customer is buying your product. Alright? Look, this is a major mistake made by even the most veteran and experienced professional salespeople. Your customer is investing in way more than just the product. Look, once they buy the product, their concern is going to be what? Who's going to take care of me? You know, would I buy a computer, a home, or an automobile refrigerator, or watch? I bought this watch. Hey, who's going to take care of it when one of the screws come out? Where am I going to go? Am I going to know where to go? I've had that experience. Where am I going to go buy this little $300 screw from? Where do I get it from? Am I going to lose the whole watch? I'm interested in more than the product. Who's going to service me, take care of me, back me? The more complicated your product, the more you must sell the rest of your company. Look, who's going to be there for your client if you're not there? Who helps that client if you aren't able to? What happens next month, next year, three years from now? Are the people in the environment friendly? Do they care? Do I know them? Do I know them by name? Have I met them? The rest of the company should be sold before a sale is ever made because this will become the difference between whether you make a sale and how much you get for that sale. Look, it doesn't matter. I'm just going to tell you a quick story here. I'm staying at a hotel while I'm here shooting this. And this hotel is not the biggest hotel in this town. It's not the nicest hotel in this town. But I'm going to tell you something. The people in there are calling me by name. They're nice to me. They try to assist me. They show me around. Look, it's the whole company, not the room I'm getting. You get it? Look, it doesn't matter whether you're selling watches, coffee, or automobiles. Starbucks, high-end watches, low-end watches. You're selling a $10,000 automobile or you're selling a $200,000 automobile. Sell the rest of the assets. You never go to war without ammo. And all the artillery, all the troops, all the technology you can. And I don't care if you like the people you work with, sell them. Okay? The more confidence your buyer has in you and your company and its ability to deliver, its ability and desire to deliver service before, during, and after the sale, the more effective you will be in the close and the more, the more you will be able to maintain your price. We're not talking about customer satisfaction here. We're talking about customer service. And customer service is before the sale. That means you need to show your customer it's not just the product. It's you, the company, the vice president, the CEO, the receptionist, the people that park cars there. Look, it's everybody. And you need to sell that and that's what we're going to talk about in this module.
